name: Upchieve
description: A nonprofit offering free, on-demand online tutoring and college counseling so students can get academic help whenever they need it.

name: ENGin
description: Pairs English-speaking volunteers with Ukrainian learners for weekly online conversation practice and cross-cultural connection.

name: Salvation Army
description: A faith-based charity providing food, shelter, and support to people in need, with volunteer roles at locations across the region.

name: Green Living Science
description: A Detroit nonprofit teaching recycling and environmental education, with volunteers helping at programs and events.

name: Trinity Health
description: A hospital system whose volunteers support patients, families, and care teams across its Michigan locations.

name: Forgotten Harvest
description: Rescues surplus food and delivers it free to families in need across metro Detroit, with volunteers sorting and distributing.

name: Oakland County Animal Shelter
description: Provides shelter and medical care to thousands of animals each year, with volunteers supporting daily care and adoptions.

name: Living & Learning Enrichment Center
description: An enrichment center supporting people with disabilities through social, vocational, and community programs that rely on volunteers.

name: Hospitality House
description: A food pantry providing groceries and assistance to neighbors in need, with respect for each person's dignity.

name: Corewell Health
description: Volunteers support patients and hospital operations across this large Southeast Michigan health system.

name: Novi Public Library
description: Teen volunteers help library staff run programs and special projects at the Novi Public Library.

name: No Kid Hungry
description: A national campaign working to end childhood hunger, with a range of ways for volunteers to help.

name: Novi Parks and Rec
description: The City of Novi's volunteer program coordinates residents to support community programs, events, and city services.

name: Maybury Farm
description: Volunteers help care for the farm animals at Maybury Farm, with daily feeding and cleaning in the morning and afternoon.

name: MLK Day of Service
description: A national day of service honoring Dr. King, with community volunteer projects organized through Points of Light

name: Fleece & Thank You
description: Makes comfort items and care packages for hospitalized children, with regular warehouse work crews and events statewide.

name: Translators without Borders
description: A global community providing volunteer language and translation support to humanitarian and nonprofit organizations.

name: SV Temple
description: Volunteers support events and activities across different areas of this Hindu temple in Novi.

name: St. James Church
description: Volunteers serve their community through this Novi church's Volunteers In Action team.

name: Brookdale Senior Living
description: Volunteers spend time with residents at senior living communities, sharing activities and companionship.

name: Detroit Opera House
description: Volunteers serve as ambassadors at the historic Detroit Opera House, welcoming patrons and supporting performances.

name: Lighthouse MI
description: An Oakland County nonprofit fighting poverty and homelessness, with individual and group volunteer projects in housing and food assistance.

name: Detroit Rescue Mission Ministries
description: Provides shelter, meals, and recovery services in Detroit, with roles in meal service, tutoring, and youth activities; welcomes youth and church groups.

name: COTS Detroit
description: Offers emergency shelter and helps families reach stable housing, with volunteers serving meals, organizing pantries, and leading activities for kids.

name: Cass Community Social Services
description: Provides food, housing, and job programs in Detroit, with volunteers helping at shelters and through its social enterprise projects.

name: Neighborhood Service Organization
description: A major Detroit provider addressing homelessness, with volunteers sorting donations, sponsoring meals, and supporting events.

name: Humble Design
description: Furnishes homes for families leaving homelessness, with volunteers collecting and refurbishing donated furniture and decorating move-in days.

name: Detroit Institute of Arts
description: A major art museum with general volunteer roles and a paid Teen Arts Council for high schoolers who create programming for other teens.

name: Arts & Scraps
description: Recycles materials into hands-on creative projects for kids, with volunteers sorting supplies and packing art and STEM kits.

name: Mosaic Youth Theatre of Detroit
description: A performing arts program where volunteers mentor young actors and singers and help with productions.

name: Metro Detroit Youth Clubs
description: Serves youth across Detroit, Royal Oak, and Southfield with tutoring, mentoring, and athletics that volunteers help deliver.

name: Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Detroit
description: Provides academic support, mentorship, and sports coaching, with volunteers serving as tutors, mentors, coaches, and event helpers.

name: Winning Futures
description: Pairs volunteer mentors with high school students to build job-readiness skills, with training before each short session.

name: Easterseals MORC Miracle League
description: Supports children and adults with disabilities, including an adaptive baseball program where volunteers act as buddies for players.

name: Detroit Zoo
description: Offers volunteer and youth programs, including a teen Counselor-in-Training track for ages 16-17 who help guide younger groups.

name: Michigan Armed Forces Hospitality Center
description: Runs hospitality lounges at Detroit Metro Airport for service members and veterans, with volunteers greeting guests and keeping lounges stocked.

name: VA Detroit Healthcare System
description: One of metro Detroit's largest hospital volunteer programs, with roles escorting patients, greeting visitors, and assisting staff.

name: Soldiers' Angels
description: Supports current and former service members and their families, with flexible Detroit-area volunteer opportunities.

name: The Mission Continues (Detroit Platoon)
description: Empowers veterans to keep serving through local projects like park and environmental restoration; open to veterans and non-veterans.

name: Learn To Be
description: Provides free one-on-one online tutoring to underserved K-12 students nationwide, tracks hours, and welcomes high-school-age tutors.

name: Schoolhouse.world
description: A global peer-tutoring community where volunteers host free online sessions in math and SAT prep and earn verifiable hours.

name: Step Up Tutoring
description: Connects volunteers with elementary and middle school students for free online math tutoring and mentorship.

name: Reading Partners
description: Online literacy tutoring where volunteers help students build reading skills about an hour a week.

name: Gleaners Community Food Bank
description: Distributes food across five Southeast Michigan counties, with volunteers sorting and packing food and supporting distributions.

name: Capuchin Soup Kitchen
description: Serves meals and provides food, clothing, and youth programs in Detroit, welcoming volunteers as young as 12 (7 at its urban farm).

name: Focus: HOPE
description: Packs monthly food boxes for tens of thousands of Detroit-area seniors, with individual and group volunteers on its assembly lines.

name: Make Food Not Waste
description: Rescues surplus food and redistributes it, with volunteers prepping and packing meals at its upcycling kitchen.

name: The Greening of Detroit
description: Plants trees and maintains green spaces in Detroit, with spring and fall planting events open to teens and families.

name: Friends of the Rouge
description: Works to restore the Rouge River through cleanups, water quality monitoring, and habitat projects supported by volunteers.

name: Huron-Clinton Metroparks
description: Volunteers support stewardship workdays and programs across the regional Metroparks system in metro Detroit.

name: American Red Cross (SE Michigan)
description: Supports disaster relief, blood drives, and military families, with in-person and virtual roles open to youth 13-17 with parental consent.

name: Henry Ford Health
description: Volunteers support patients and staff across this health system's Southeast Michigan hospitals and campuses.

name: Ronald McDonald House Charities Michigan
description: Supports families of hospitalized children, with volunteers helping with meals, activities, and house operations.

name: Gilda's Club (Cancer Support Community)
description: Provides free support for people affected by cancer, with volunteers leading activities and programs at its locations.

name: Crisis Text Line
description: A 24/7 text support service where trained volunteers, who must be 18 or older, help people through moments of crisis from home.

name: 7 Cups
description: An online emotional support platform where trained volunteer listeners, including a teen listener tier, offer a caring ear.

name: Smithsonian Transcription Center
description: Volunteers age 14 and up transcribe historical documents online to make Smithsonian collections searchable.

name: Library of Congress (By the People)
description: Anyone can transcribe and review digitized historical documents online to improve their searchability and access.

name: National Archives Citizen Archivist
description: Volunteers transcribe and tag federal records online to make the National Archives catalog more accessible.

name: Zooniverse
description: A people-powered research platform where volunteers help classify real scientific data on projects from astronomy to ecology.

name: Cards for Hospitalized Kids
description: Volunteers make and mail handmade cards to bring encouragement to hospitalized children nationwide.

name: Letters Against Isolation
description: Volunteers write and send handwritten letters to combat loneliness among isolated seniors.

name: Color A Smile
description: Volunteers color cheerful drawings that are mailed to seniors, troops, and others who could use a smile.

name: Operation Gratitude
description: Volunteers write letters and assemble care-package items for military members, veterans, and first responders.

name: Be My Eyes
description: A free app connecting volunteers with blind and low-vision users who need quick visual help through live video.

name: Tarjimly
description: Connects volunteer translators and interpreters with refugees, immigrants, and the aid workers serving them.

name: UN Online Volunteers
description: Offers remote, skills-based tasks supporting development and humanitarian organizations around the world.

name: Catchafire
description: Matches volunteers with nonprofits for skills-based projects in areas like design, writing, and marketing.

name: Michigan Humane
description: One of the state's largest animal welfare organizations, with shelter and event roles and youth options for ages 14+ accompanied by an adult.

name: Leader Dogs for the Blind
description: Trains guide dogs for people who are blind or low vision, with on-campus volunteer roles (18+) and a separate puppy-raising program.

name: Humane Society of Huron Valley
description: An Ann Arbor animal shelter with general roles for ages 18+ and a Junior Volunteer program for ages 12-17.

name: Michigan Animal Rescue League
description: A no-kill shelter in Pontiac where volunteers support animal care and adoptions.

name: Detroit Dog Rescue
description: A Detroit rescue and shelter where volunteers help care for dogs and support adoptions.

name: Friendship Circle
description: Builds friendships between teen volunteers (7th-12th grade) and children with special needs through social and recreational programs.

name: Yad Ezra
description: A kosher food pantry serving Jewish families in need across Southeast Michigan, with volunteers packing and sorting food.

name: Catholic Charities of Southeast Michigan
description: Provides counseling, food, and family services to thousands across the region, with a range of volunteer roles.

name: Michigan Science Center
description: Volunteers help run exhibits and engage visitors at this Detroit science museum.

name: DAPCEP
description: The Detroit Area Pre-College Engineering Program, where volunteers support STEM events and courses for K-12 students.

name: FIRST in Michigan
description: Supports student robotics teams statewide, with volunteers mentoring teams and helping run competitions.

name: Ann Arbor Hands-On Museum
description: An interactive science museum where volunteers assist on the floor and at programs and events.

name: SciStarter
description: An online hub that connects volunteers to thousands of citizen-science projects they can join from anywhere.

name: NASA Citizen Science
description: Lets volunteers contribute to real NASA research projects online, from spotting planets to tracking clouds.

name: iNaturalist
description: Volunteers record and help identify wildlife observations, contributing to a global biodiversity database.

name: Cranbrook Institute of Science
description: A science museum in Bloomfield Hills where volunteers support galleries, programs, and events.

name: Habitat for Humanity Detroit
description: Builds and repairs homes alongside families in need, with volunteers helping on construction sites and at its ReStore home-improvement shops.

name: Big Brothers Big Sisters of SE Michigan
description: Matches adult volunteer mentors with children ages 8-18 across Washtenaw, Oakland, Macomb, and Wayne counties for one-to-one mentoring.

name: Special Olympics Michigan
description: Provides year-round sports training and competition for people with intellectual disabilities, with volunteers serving as coaches, event helpers, and Unified partners.

name: Best Buddies Michigan
description: Builds one-to-one friendships between volunteers and people with intellectual and developmental disabilities, with school chapters that welcome teen members.

name: Habitat for Humanity Oakland County
description: Builds affordable housing and runs ReStore shops in Oakland County, with volunteers helping on builds and in the stores.

name: Meals on Wheels (Detroit Area Agency on Aging)
description: Delivers daily meals and a friendly check-in to homebound seniors and adults with disabilities, with volunteers driving routes and delivering meals.

name: Belle Isle Conservancy
description: Protects and restores Detroit's Belle Isle park, with volunteers greeting aquarium visitors, removing litter, and helping with habitat projects.

name: Detroit Riverfront Conservancy
description: Maintains and programs Detroit's riverfront and greenway, with volunteers supporting cleanups, gardening, and public events.

name: Keep Growing Detroit
description: Supports urban farming and gardens across Detroit, with volunteers helping at the Eastern Market farm and community growing sites.

name: LibriVox
description: A worldwide volunteer project that records public-domain books into free audiobooks, open to anyone with a computer and microphone.

name: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap (Missing Maps)
description: Volunteers map unmapped areas online to help humanitarian groups respond to disasters and crises around the world.

name: Project Gutenberg (Distributed Proofreaders)
description: Volunteers proofread pages of scanned books online to help turn them into free public-domain ebooks.

name: iMentor
description: Pairs volunteer mentors with high school students online to support their path to college and career, with weekly digital check-ins.

name: CoachArt
description: Volunteers teach free virtual lessons in arts and athletics to children affected by chronic illness and their siblings.

name: Matrix Human Services
description: A large Detroit human-services nonprofit running Head Start, youth, and community programs that volunteers help support.

name: Alternatives for Girls
description: Supports homeless and at-risk girls and young women in Detroit, with volunteers helping in mentoring, tutoring, and program activities.

name: The Heat and Warmth Fund (THAW)
description: Helps Michigan families facing utility shutoffs stay warm, with volunteers assisting at outreach events and resource fairs.

name: Detroit Historical Society
description: Volunteers serve as greeters, tour guides, and event helpers at the Detroit Historical Museum and Dossin Great Lakes Museum.

name: Sierra Club Michigan
description: The state chapter of the national environmental group, with volunteers helping on conservation campaigns, outings, and a youth-focused coalition.
